Scale.NoEffect: Target is two or more Scale levels above: no effect, the result is a normal Failure.
Scale.TooSmall: Target is one Scale level above: regular Successes count as Failures.
Scale.CritOnly: Target is one Scale level above: only this Critical can shiftdown its Traits.
Scale.CritExhausts: You are one Scale level above: this Critical fully Exhausts a target Trait.
Scale.NoEffectTrait: {trait} is two or more Scale levels above your roll — no effect.
Scale.TooSmallTrait: {trait} is one Scale level above your roll — only a Critical Success lands.
Scale.ScaleUpHint: Shiftdown a Core Trait to treat the linked Focus Trait at a higher Scale for this roll.
Scale.XpBoostChat: {actor} spends {xp} XP — this roll counts as Scale {scale}.
Scale.AtMax: This roll is already at Scale 4.
Scale.ScaledUpAlready: This roll has already been Scaled Up.
Scale.ScaledUpUnavailable: No available Scaled Up Technique matches the Traits rolled.
Scale.ScaledUpUseHint: Apply {trait}'s Scaled Up from the "Scale Up" button on an Action Roll.
Scale.ScaledUpPickCore: Scale Up {focus} — choose a Core Trait to shiftdown
Scale.ScaledUpCoreHint: Shiftdown a Core Trait to treat the Focus as a higher Scale.
Scale.BindFocusHint: Pick the Focus Trait this Scaled Up Technique upgrades.

Compendium.ScaleUpDesc: After rolling the linked Focus Trait, shift one of your Core Traits down to treat the Focus at a higher Scale for that roll — letting you affect targets larger than yourself. Usable once per Safe Rest or session, depending on its recharge.

Trait.BodyDesc: Your physical Trait: strength, speed, toughness and everything your body does. Roll Body for feats of force, athletics, endurance and standing your ground.
Trait.MindDesc: Your mental Trait: reason, perception, knowledge and focus. Roll Mind to investigate, recall, plan, solve problems and keep your cool.
Trait.SoulDesc: Your inner and social Trait: presence, willpower, empathy and instinct. Roll Soul to persuade, resist fear, sense trouble and connect with others.

Party.WorkInProgress: Work in progress — the party sheet is still under construction.
Party.DropHint: Drag characters here to add them to the party.
Party.CannotAdd: That actor can't be added to a party.
Party.DeployHint: Place a token for every member on the current scene.
Party.RecallHint: Remove the members' tokens from the current scene.
Party.NothingToRecall: No member tokens to recall on this scene.
Party.NoScene: Open a scene first to deploy the party.
Party.NoPermission: You don't have permission to place tokens on this scene.
Party.AddMember: Drag an Actor (or a folder) to add to the party

Keybind.OpenHint: Open the obvious party — the one holding your character, or the active party you chose.
Keybind.SetActiveHint: Choose which party the Open-Party hotkey and scene button target.

Party.DropVehicle: Drop a Vehicle here — its crew mirrors the roster
Party.NotVehicle: Only a Vehicle can be set as the active vehicle.
Party.NotLocation: Only a Location can be set as the party's location.
Party.DropLocation: Drop a Location here to set where the party is
Party.UnsafeLandmarkBlock: {landmark} is Unsafe — a Safe Rest isn't possible here. SHIFT+click Safe Rest to override.
Party.GrantXpHint: Award XP — choose the amount and which characters (uncapped).

RequestRoll.Hint: Ask a player to make an Action Roll (you set the allowed Traits and whether it's Risky or Inspired).
RequestRoll.Title: Request an Action Roll
RequestRoll.TraitsHint: Tick the Traits the player may roll. Tick none to allow all of them.
RequestRoll.Sent: Requested an Action Roll from {actor}'s player(s).
RequestRoll.NoOwner: No online player owns that character — opening the roll here instead.

Party.SafeRestHint: Run a Safe Rest for every member (SHIFT+click to ignore an Unsafe landmark).
Party.UnsafeRestHint: Run an Unsafe Rest for every member.
Party.SafeRestConfirm: Run a Safe Rest for all {count} member(s)?
Party.UnsafeRestConfirm: Run an Unsafe Rest for all {count} member(s)?

Codex.Label: in codex
Codex.NotesUnavailable: Shared notes aren't ready yet — ask the GM to open the party once.
Codex.Already: That entry is already in the codex.
Codex.PartlyHidden: Some details are hidden from players
Codex.NotesPlaceholder: What the party knows — weaknesses, behaviour, where it was met…

Quests.ReorderHint: Drag a quest onto another to reorder.
Quests.Empty: No quests yet. Add one to track the party's goals.
Quests.NotesPlaceholder: Details, stakes, what's needed to advance…
Quests.ShiftsDown: rolling fills the clock a step
Quests.ShiftToggle: Toggle whether rolling shifts the die down
Quests.LinkHint: Drag an Actor, a Codex foe, or a relic Trait onto this card to link it.
Quests.RevealHint: What players see on this quest (the die-clock is always visible).

Notes.JournalPlaceholder: What happened last session, group decisions, open hooks…
Notes.ContactsPlaceholder: Who owes the party favors, allies, debts…

Roll.TypeHint: Risky: every die that misses a success shifts down. Inspired: any success counts as a Critical Success.

Exert.Title: Exert
Exert.Confirm: Shift {trait} ({die}) down one step to automatically succeed?

Temporary.Title: Temporary Trait
Temporary.NameLabel: What does your character pull from the Pack?
Temporary.Chat: {actor} digs into the Pack and gains the temporary Trait {name} ({die}). It lasts until Exhausted or until the next Safe Rest.

Rest.SafeChat: {actor} takes a Safe Rest. All Traits return to their Max Die and Techniques refresh.
Rest.UnsafeChat: {actor} takes an Unsafe Rest. {pack} shifts down to {die}.
Rest.UnsafeHint: Shift {pack} ({die}) down one step to restore one Trait to its Max Die.
Rest.WealthHint: Challenging rests: pick the Location's Wealth die to cap recovery, or no limit.
Rest.ChallengingHint: Choose how to spend this rest.
Rest.TwoOnce: Two Traits shift up once each
Rest.NothingToRestore: Every Trait is already at its Max Die.
Rest.AllRestored: All Traits restored to their Max Die.
Rest.ResourceHint: Choose which die to spend on this rest.
Rest.CargoSharedHint: Each crew member aboard may restore one Trait to its Max Die from this same shiftdown.
Rest.CrewRestored: {actor} restocks from the Cargo: {trait} returns to its Max Die.
Rest.NotCrew: None of your characters are part of this crew.
Rest.RechargedAll: {actor}: every Trait recharged to its Max Die.

Death.Title: Core Trait Exhausted
Death.Prompt: {actor}'s {trait} is Exhausted. Take a severe Drawback and reset the die to D12, or go out in a blaze of glory?
Death.DrawbackChat: {actor} pushes through: {trait} returns at D12 with the Drawback "{drawback}".
Death.HeroicChat: {actor} chooses a heroic end. Describe the final scene; the last action is an automatic Critical Success.

CritBonus.OwnHint: Shift one of your own Traits up one step (never above its Max Die).
CritBonus.AllyHint: Shift a willing ally's Trait up one step.
CritBonus.EnemyHint: Shift the chosen target's Trait down ONE additional step, on top of the base attack. At a larger Scale a Critical Exhausts the Trait outright.
CritBonus.NarrativeHint: Work with the GM to add a cool twist: remove a Drawback, learn something useful, open an opportunity.
CritBonus.NarrativeChat: {actor} earns a narrative boost! Work out a cool extra effect with the GM.

Combat.TurnOrderHint: Pick one Core Trait and roll only its die. Success: act before the Adversaries. Failure: act after them. This is a real Action Roll: it can crit and it can shift your die down.
Combat.FirstPhase: Acts in Action Phase 1 (before the Adversaries).
Combat.SecondPhase: Acts in Action Phase 2 (after the Adversaries).
Combat.RerollHint: Players: roll the turn order for the new round.
Combat.NotYourTurnOrder: You do not own {name}; ask their player to roll the turn order.
Combat.AllExhausted: {name} has no usable Trait for the turn order roll and falls to the Second Action Phase.
Combat.AdvantageHint: Does one side have the advantage? That side acts first this round only.
Combat.AdvantagePlayersChat: The players have the advantage and act first!
Combat.AdvantageAdversariesChat: The adversaries have the advantage and act first!
Combat.NothingToRoll: No owned characters waiting to roll.

Special.Hint: Add a Special Trait preset (does not count toward the Trait budget).
Special.ArmoredDesc: Exhaust one additional Trait to overcome this Adversary. This Trait is not rolled and does not count toward the overcome total.
Special.HeavilyArmoredDesc: Exhaust two additional Traits to overcome this Adversary, and this Trait must be Exhausted before any other Trait can be shifted down.
Special.SmallGroupDesc: This Adversary takes one extra action each round and requires one additional Trait to be Exhausted.
Special.LargeGroupDesc: This Adversary takes two extra actions each round and requires two additional Traits to be Exhausted.

Session.Title: New Session
Session.Started: New session: Techniques refreshed and session XP counters reset.
Session.Prompt: Has a new session started? This resets every character's accumulated session XP and refreshes their Techniques.

Drop.NeedsTrait: Drop Keywords and Drawbacks onto a Trait card.
Drop.NoLinkedItem: No Keyword/Drawback item with this name. Create one to link a description.

ItemSheet.AutoShift: Auto shift down on max roll
ItemSheet.DescriptorHint: Drag this item onto a Trait card to attach it.

Warnings.TraitExhaustedNamed: {trait} is Exhausted and cannot be rolled.
Warnings.PackExhausted: The Pack Trait is Exhausted or missing.
Warnings.NotOwner: You do not own this character.
Warnings.NoTarget: Target or select a token first.
Warnings.MustExhaustFirst: This Trait must be Exhausted before the others.
Warnings.NeedsActor: This Trait must belong to an Actor to be rolled.
Warnings.BlockedBy: {traits} must be Exhausted first (Ctrl+click to override).
Warnings.NoGM: No active GM to apply this action.

Tooltips.Roll: Roll this Trait (Shift+click: quick roll without the dialog)
Tooltips.ShiftUp: Shiftup: improve this die one step (never past the Max Die)
Tooltips.ShiftDown: Shiftdown: worsen this die one step (D12 becomes Exhausted; Ctrl+click ignores Exhausted First)
Tooltips.Exert: Exert: shift this die down voluntarily to succeed automatically (+1 XP)
Tooltips.Restore: Restore this Trait to its Max Die
Tooltips.MaxDie: The best die this Trait can reach
Tooltips.Temporary: Temporary Trait: disappears on the next Safe Rest
Tooltips.MakeTemporary: Shift the Pack down one step to improvise a temporary Focus Trait
Tooltips.UnsafeRest: Unsafe Rest: shift the Pack down to restore another Trait
Tooltips.SafeRest: Safe Rest: restore every Trait and refresh Techniques
Tooltips.XpSession: XP earned this session / session cap
Tooltips.Power: Power: sets actions per round and Traits needed to overcome
Tooltips.Actions: Action Rolls this Adversary makes each round
Tooltips.Defeat: Exhausted counting Traits / total required to overcome
Tooltips.DefeatCounts: Exhausting this Trait counts toward overcoming
Tooltips.ExtraRequired: Additional Traits that must be Exhausted
Tooltips.MustFirst: Must be Exhausted before other Traits can shift down
Tooltips.UseTechnique: Spend one use and post to chat
Tooltips.Lock: Lock: hide configuration and show the pretty view
Tooltips.EditToggle: Edit mode: unlock names, Keywords/Drawbacks and structure
Tooltips.NoCount: Exhausting this Trait does not count toward overcoming the Adversary
Tooltips.LockTrait: Lock structural editing of this Trait
Tooltips.RechargeAll: Recharge every Trait to its Max Die

RestMode.Name: Rest & Recovery Mode
RestMode.Hint: Standard: Unsafe Rest restores one Trait. Simple: Unsafe Rest restores everything and sessions start fresh. Challenging: slower recovery and Safe Rests capped by Location Wealth.

CritRule.Name: Critical Success Rule
CritRule.Hint: Standard: any die showing 1 crits. All Ones: every die must show 1. Every One: each 1 grants its own bonus.

XpLimit.Name: Session XP Cap
XpLimit.Hint: Maximum XP a character can earn from shiftdowns and exerts in a single session.

AutoXp.Name: Automatic XP
AutoXp.Hint: Grant XP automatically when dice are forced to shiftdown or a character exerts.

RerollTurnOrder.Name: Re-roll Turn Order Each Round
RerollTurnOrder.Hint: Clear player initiatives at the start of every round so the turn order is rolled again.

PromptCoreExhausted.Name: Core Trait Exhaustion Prompt
PromptCoreExhausted.Hint: When a character's Core Trait becomes Exhausted, offer the Drawback / Heroic Death choice.

EnableScale.Name: Enable the Scale System
EnableScale.Hint: Scale is an optional rule. Turn it off to hide every Scale control, badge and interaction across the system.

EnableScaledUp.Name: Optional Rule: Scaled Up Technique
EnableScaledUp.Hint: Allow the Scaled Up Technique type, a Technique bound to a Focus Trait that adds a "Scale Up" button to its Action Roll cards.

EnableXpScaleUp.Name: Optional Rule: Spend XP to Scale Up
EnableXpScaleUp.Hint: Add a right-click chat option to spend Banked XP and raise a roll's Scale.

XpPerScaleStep.Name: XP per Scale step
XpPerScaleStep.Hint: Banked XP spent for each +1 Scale via the right-click Scale Up option.

PromptSession.Name: Ask to start a session on login
PromptSession.Hint: When the GM logs in, ask whether a new session has begun (resets accumulated session XP for every character in the world). Resets daily.

XpCost.CoreDie: XP Cost: Improve Core Max Die
XpCost.Hint: Cost shown on the advancement chips of the character sheet.

ActionHud.Name: Action HUD
ActionHud.Hint: Show a quick bar with the controlled token's Traits and Techniques at the bottom of the screen.

HudAlwaysOpen.Name: Keep Action HUD always open
HudAlwaysOpen.Hint: If you have a character assigned to your user, keep its Action HUD on-screen even when no token is selected on the canvas.

HudMenuTrigger.Name: Action HUD submenus open on
HudMenuTrigger.Hint: Whether the Action HUD's submenus open when you click the group button or when you hover over it.

Advancements.Name: XP Advancements
Advancements.Hint: What XP can buy and how much it costs. Shown as chips on the character sheet.
Advancements.Label: Advancement

ScaleMenu.Name: Scale Rules
ScaleMenu.Hint: The optional Scale system: enable it, allow Scaled Up Techniques, and spend XP to Scale Up.

ActionHudMenu.Name: Action HUD
ActionHudMenu.Hint: Your personal Action HUD: turn it on, keep it always open, and choose how its submenus open.

RangesMenu.Name: Distance Ranges
RangesMenu.Hint: Customize the abstract distance bands and what the on-canvas hover readout shows.

StatusEffects.Name: Status Effects
StatusEffects.Hint: The color markers that replace Foundry's default conditions. Each one is a colored badge you can toggle on a token from its HUD. Edit the name and color of each, add your own, or restore the defaults.
StatusEffects.Label: Status name

Theme.Name: Theme
Theme.Hint: Light or dark appearance for all SHIFT sheets and chat messages.

Recharge.CustomHint: No trigger selected: this Technique only recharges manually.
Recharge.AtWillHint: At Will — unlimited use, no recharge needed.

Target.ApplyHint: Pick one of the target's Traits and shiftdown.
Target.AlreadyApplied: This roll's effect has already landed on a target.
Target.RetargetHint: Pick a different target for this roll (or target a token first).

Assist.Title: Assist
Assist.Hint: Opt in to help: pick one of your Traits and roll it. A success supports the action; rolling the maximum still shifts your die down.
Assist.Success: {actor} lends a hand: the assist succeeds!
Assist.Failure: {actor} tries to help, but the assist falls short.
Assist.NeedActor: Select your token or assign a character to assist.
Assist.NotSelf: You cannot assist your own roll.

Vehicle.CrewHint: Drag a Character here to crew this Vehicle.

Clocks.Title: Global Traits
Clocks.AddHint: Pin a free-floating Trait to the table — everyone sees it update live.
Clocks.ShiftsDown: Shift down on a max roll
Clocks.ShiftOnTip: A max roll shifts this Trait down — click to disable
Clocks.ShiftOffTip: A max roll won't shift this Trait down — click to enable
Clocks.Move: Drag to move · drop over the Players list to dock

Group.Title: Work Together
Group.Invite: {actor} wants to work together using {trait} ({die}). One ally can join with any Trait — Core not required. A Critical needs both dice showing 1.
Group.Hint: Pick any of your Traits — this is the only roll that can combine two Focus or Pack Traits.
Group.NeedActor: Select your token or assign a character to join.
Group.NotSelf: You cannot join your own roll.

Browser.Title: SHIFT Browser
Browser.Truncated: More results available — refine the search.

Hud.PackShiftHint: Shift+click: spend for a temporary Focus Trait
Hud.ExertHint: Ctrl+click: Exert (shift down for an automatic success)

Macro.NoCharacters: No characters you own were found.
Macro.GMOnly: Only the GM can do that.
Macro.GrantXpHint: Choose who receives XP and how much.

Location.SafeToggle: Safe — click to mark as Unsafe
Location.UnsafeToggle: Unsafe — click to mark as Safe
Location.LandmarksHint: Significant areas of the Location. Safe Landmarks allow Safe Rests; Unsafe ones make resting dangerous.
Location.NPCsHint: Drag any Actor here to tie it to this Location as an NPC.
